title: rubus tomentosus subf. sterilis
intro: Rubus tomentosus subf. sterilis, also known as the sterile downy raspberry, is a rare and lesser-known species of raspberry that belongs to the Rubus genus and is native to the eastern and central regions of North America. It is primarily valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Rubus tomentosus subf. sterilis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
